title: Dubai Chocolate Bar
tagline: The viral pistachio-kunafa chocolate that broke the internet.
description: Homemade version of the Fix Dessert Chocolatier chocolate bar that went viral in 2024. Layers of crunchy kunafa (kataifi), tahini-pistachio filling, and dark chocolate.
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Toss kataifi with melted butter, spread on baking sheet. Bake 10-12 min until golden and crispy. Let cool, then crumble into small pieces.
Mix tahini, honey, salt, and rose water in a bowl. Fold in chopped pistachios and half the crispy kataifi. The mixture should be thick but spreadable.
Melt chocolate in a double boiler or microwave (30-second intervals, stirring between).
Line a 9x5 loaf pan with parchment. Pour half the chocolate into the pan, spread evenly. Refrigerate 5 min until just set.
Spread the pistachio-tahini mixture evenly over the chocolate layer. Press remaining kataifi crumbles on top.
Pour remaining melted chocolate over the top, spreading to cover completely. Tap pan to release air bubbles.
Refrigerate at least 2 hours until fully set. Slice into bars with a sharp knife dipped in hot water.
notes: Kataifi pastry is found in Middle Eastern grocery stores or the freezer section. Shredded phyllo works too.
For extra crunch, toast the pistachios lightly before chopping.
Bars keep refrigerated for 2 weeks. Let sit 5 min at room temp before eating for best texture.
